task_completion.description: Verify the Electrical Engineering Calculations app is launched and the RMS to Peak conversion tool is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the interface of the Electrical Engineering Calculations app, specifically on a screen or section for converting RMS voltage to Peak voltage.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct RMS voltage value is entered into the app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The number '120' is visible in the input text field designated for RMS Voltage.

task_completion.description: Verify the app successfully calculates and displays the Peak Voltage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app displays a resulting Peak Voltage value. The value should be approximately 169.7 (any value between 168.2 and 171.2 is acceptable).

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot of the result screen is saved to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., using 'screencap') or a file manager view showing the creation of 'rms_to_peak_result.png' inside the '/sdcard/tasks/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the resulting Peak Voltage value is written to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., 'echo 169.7 > /sdcard/tasks/peak_voltage.txt') or a text editor showing the calculated number being saved to '/sdcard/tasks/peak_voltage.txt'.

integrity.description: Ensure the calculation was performed using the requested Electrical Engineering Calculations app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the actual Android app interface for the calculation, not a web browser, a generic calculator, or a Python script.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just write the expected answer without performing the app calculation.
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be at least one screenshot showing the app actively displaying the calculated result of ~169.7V before the text file is created.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot saved is an actual capture of the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The command used to save the screenshot should be a standard screen capture command (like 'screencap -p') rather than downloading or moving an unrelated image file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input RMS voltage is 120V. The correct Peak Voltage is calculated as 120 × √2 ≈ 169.7V. Due to a ±1.5V tolerance, accept any displayed or written value between 168.2 and 171.2. The output screenshot must be named 'rms_to_peak_result.png' and the text file must be 'peak_voltage.txt', both located in '/sdcard/tasks/'.
